message:
fix(scheduler): correct test name to match actual assertion logic

Rename uniform_posterior_prefers_aggressive to
uniform_posterior_prefers_conservative — the test body asserts that
a uniform prior with E[aggressive]=17.0 > E[balanced]=9.0 >
E[conservative]=6.75 should prefer conservative (lower expected cost),
which the old test name contradicted.

Also sync .beads/issues.jsonl with current bead state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1421,7 +1421,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.395","title":"[audit][link] Self-link establishment must not duplicate self-exit actions","description":"Focused audit follow-up for src/link.rs only. LinkSet::establish_with_policy() currently allows task_a == task_b and indexes the same LinkRef twice in task_index. That makes a single logical self-link appear twice in peers_of()/peers_with_policy() and causes resolve_exits() to emit duplicate self-exit actions for one abnormal termination. Scope: make self-links fail closed or otherwise canonicalize them so one logical self-link cannot duplicate peer/exit behavior, add focused regressions, and keep the write surface to src/link.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseIsland","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","updated_at":"2026-03-17T01:14:19.447183099Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T01:14:19.447153464Z","close_reason":"Patched src/link.rs so self-links are indexed once in task_index, eliminating duplicate peers_with_policy/resolve_exits behavior. Added regressions self_link_is_indexed_once_in_peer_views and resolve_exits_self_link_emits_single_action. Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-roseisland-link-test cargo test -p asupersync self_link --lib passed. cargo fmt --check is blocked by unrelated formatting drift in asupersync-tokio-compat/src/body_bridge.rs; cargo check --all-targets is blocked by unrelated tests/e2e_raptorq_pipeline.rs API drift; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings is blocked by unrelated src/process.rs if_not_else lint.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","link","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.395","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.396","title":"[audit][link] Self-link establishment must not duplicate self-exit actions","description":"Focused audit follow-up for src/link.rs only. LinkSet::establish_with_policy() currently allows task_a == task_b and indexes the same LinkRef twice in task_index. That makes a single logical self-link appear twice in peers_of()/peers_with_policy() and causes resolve_exits() to emit duplicate self-exit actions for one abnormal termination. Scope: make self-links fail closed or otherwise canonicalize them so one logical self-link cannot duplicate peer/exit behavior, add focused regressions, and keep the write surface to src/link.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ChartreuseBeaver","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:34:22.264563078Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:34:22.264535207Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current tree; stale-open after prior audit/fix landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","link","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.396","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.397","title":"[audit][reactor] Registration::deregister must preserve Drop cleanup after persistent deregister failure","description":"Fresh diff audit of src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs found that Registration::deregister(self) consumed the handle but still disarmed Drop even when both explicit deregister attempts failed or the reactor panicked. That leaves callers with no retry surface and can strand reactor bookkeeping instead of preserving one final best-effort cleanup pass. Patch should keep Drop armed on persistent explicit deregister failure, add focused regression coverage, and validate with rch-offloaded cargo commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaStone","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:16:02.736764796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T02:31:08.359563488Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T02:31:08.359534123Z","close_reason":"Completed: kept Registration Drop armed on persistent explicit deregister failure/panic, added focused regressions, validated via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","reactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.397","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:16:02.736764796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2524,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.397","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Patched src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs so Registration::deregister(self) only disarms Drop on successful explicit cleanup. Previously the consumed handle still disarmed Drop after persistent deregister error or panic, which removed the last best-effort cleanup path and could strand reactor bookkeeping.

Validation:
- git diff --check passed
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-fuchsiastone-registration cargo check -p asupersync --lib passed
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-fuchsiastone-registration cargo test -p asupersync --lib explicit_deregister_ -- --nocapture passed with 5 tests
- rch exec -- rustfmt --check src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs passed after fixing import ordering
- focused cargo clippy on the lib is currently blocked by unrelated concurrent DNS work in src/net/dns/resolver.rs:259 (clippy needless pass by value), not by this patch
- repo-wide cargo fmt --check is currently red because concurrent edits in src/net/dns/cache.rs and src/net/dns/resolver.rs are not yet rustfmt-clean

Regression coverage:
- strengthened persistent-error test to prove Drop stays armed and performs the final cleanup pass
- strengthened panic-path test to prove the post-error Drop retry occurs
- added explicit_deregister_error_still_allows_drop_cleanup_success, where the first two explicit deregister attempts fail and the final Drop cleanup attempt succeeds","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:30:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","title":"[audit][dns] Canonicalize trailing-dot FQDNs in resolver/cache","description":"Root cause:
- resolver host validation rejects standards-valid absolute hostnames when the only extra byte is the trailing root dot
- cache key normalization lowercases hosts but does not canonicalize a trailing dot, so example.com and example.com. fragment cache/negative-cache behavior

Planned fix:
- canonicalize DNS cache/resolver host keys so trailing-dot FQDNs share cache entries with the non-dotted form
- accept max-length absolute hostnames by validating the canonical hostname length instead of the raw dotted input
- add focused regressions for cache aliasing and absolute-hostname length handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseHawk","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123970759Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123946414Z","close_reason":"Completed: current HEAD contains the DNS absolute-name cache fix plus regressions; actual bug was trailing-dot cache aliasing in resolver lookup/store, while strict clippy/fmt failures remain outside this slice.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","dns"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2552,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"TurquoiseHawk","text":"Fresh-eyes DNS audit update:
- confirmed the live bug is the opposite of the bead title: `Resolver::lookup_ip()` was stripping one trailing root dot before cache lookup/store, which aliased `name.` onto `name` and could leak search-domain-sensitive cache results across the absolute/non-absolute boundary
- kept trailing-dot trimming only for hostname-length validation in `do_lookup_ip()`; cache identity now stays on the original query host
- added focused regressions in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` proving dotted queries do not reuse non-dotted positive or negative cache entries, plus a `src/net/dns/cache.rs` regression proving case-insensitive lookups still preserve dotted-vs-non-dotted key separation

Validation so far:
- `git diff --check -- src/net/dns/resolver.rs src/net/dns/cache.rs` PASS
- `ubs --only=rust src/net/dns` ran clean of new critical findings but is noisy on existing test unwrap/assert inventory and one unrelated lifecycle heuristic in resolver connect code
- `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets` reached remote `exit=0`; local tail then reported the usual artifact-retrieval vanished-file warning
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` is red outside this slice in `src/http/h1/http_client.rs`, `src/net/websocket/*`, and `tests/websocket_acceptor_trailing_bytes.rs`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` is red outside this slice in `src/stream/debounce.rs` and `src/net/websocket/handshake.rs`
- focused `rch` test lanes for the new DNS regressions are still running unusually slowly / quietly after compile on the remote worker; no DNS-specific failure has surfaced yet","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:20:33Z"},{"id":2544,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Start note:
- `bv --robot-triage` and `bv --robot-next` both timed out again in this repo, so I used the ready set plus live code inspection to keep momentum.
- Concrete bug is in DNS canonicalization, not another stale already-landed bead.

Root cause under fix:
- trailing-dot FQDNs fragment resolver/cache behavior because cache key normalization only lowercases
- resolver length validation checks the raw host string before canonicalizing away the trailing root dot","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:28Z"},{"id":2545,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Scope correction after deeper DNS semantics review:
- I am **not** canonicalizing trailing-dot and non-dotted names into one cache key; that would risk changing search-domain semantics.
- The concrete live bug is narrower and real: `Resolver::do_lookup_ip()` rejected a standards-valid max-length absolute hostname because it counted the trailing root dot before validation.
- Fix in flight keeps query semantics intact and only validates length against the hostname with one trailing root dot trimmed.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:01:58Z"},{"id":2546,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Progress update:
- landed the resolver-side fix in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs`: max-length absolute hostnames now validate after trimming one trailing root dot for length-check purposes only
- added focused regressions for the standards-valid 254-byte absolute hostname and the still-invalid overlong case

Validation status:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/net/dns/resolver.rs` passed
- `ubs src/net/dns/resolver.rs` exited 0
- `rch exec -- ... cargo test --lib absolute_hostname -- --nocapture` and `rch exec -- ... cargo check --all-targets` are currently blocked by infrastructure, not code: the scheduler repeatedly routes to worker `vmi1227854`, and `rch fleet status` reports that worker at `0MB` free on `/tmp`, producing `cc` temp-file `ENOSPC` failures before compilation finishes

Reservation narrowed:
- released `src/net/dns/cache.rs`; keeping `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` reserved while the offloaded cargo gates are blocked","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:06:58Z"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","title":"[audit][dns] Canonicalize trailing-dot FQDNs in resolver/cache","description":"Root cause:
- resolver host validation rejects standards-valid absolute hostnames when the only extra byte is the trailing root dot
- cache key normalization lowercases hosts but does not canonicalize a trailing dot, so example.com and example.com. fragment cache/negative-cache behavior

Planned fix:
- canonicalize DNS cache/resolver host keys so trailing-dot FQDNs share cache entries with the non-dotted form
- accept max-length absolute hostnames by validating the canonical hostname length instead of the raw dotted input
- add focused regressions for cache aliasing and absolute-hostname length handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseHawk","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123970759Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123946414Z","close_reason":"Completed: current HEAD contains the DNS absolute-name cache fix plus regressions; actual bug was trailing-dot cache aliasing in resolver lookup/store, while strict clippy/fmt failures remain outside this slice.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","dns"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2544,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Start note:

Root cause under fix:
- trailing-dot FQDNs fragment resolver/cache behavior because cache key normalization only lowercases
- resolver length validation checks the raw host string before canonicalizing away the trailing root dot","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:28Z"},{"id":2545,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Scope correction after deeper DNS semantics review:
- I am **not** canonicalizing trailing-dot and non-dotted names into one cache key; that would risk changing search-domain semantics.
- The concrete live bug is narrower and real: `Resolver::do_lookup_ip()` rejected a standards-valid max-length absolute hostname because it counted the trailing root dot before validation.
- Fix in flight keeps query semantics intact and only validates length against the hostname with one trailing root dot trimmed.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:01:58Z"},{"id":2546,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Progress update:
- landed the resolver-side fix in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs`: max-length absolute hostnames now validate after trimming one trailing root dot for length-check purposes only
- added focused regressions for the standards-valid 254-byte absolute hostname and the still-invalid overlong case

Validation status:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/net/dns/resolver.rs` passed
- `ubs src/net/dns/resolver.rs` exited 0
- `rch exec -- ... cargo test --lib absolute_hostname -- --nocapture` and `rch exec -- ... cargo check --all-targets` are currently blocked by infrastructure, not code: the scheduler repeatedly routes to worker `vmi1227854`, and `rch fleet status` reports that worker at `0MB` free on `/tmp`, producing `cc` temp-file `ENOSPC` failures before compilation finishes

Reservation narrowed:
- released `src/net/dns/cache.rs`; keeping `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` reserved while the offloaded cargo gates are blocked","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:06:58Z"},{"id":2552,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"TurquoiseHawk","text":"Fresh-eyes DNS audit update:
- confirmed the live bug is the opposite of the bead title: `Resolver::lookup_ip()` was stripping one trailing root dot before cache lookup/store, which aliased `name.` onto `name` and could leak search-domain-sensitive cache results across the absolute/non-absolute boundary
- kept trailing-dot trimming only for hostname-length validation in `do_lookup_ip()`; cache identity now stays on the original query host
- added focused regressions in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` proving dotted queries do not reuse non-dotted positive or negative cache entries, plus a `src/net/dns/cache.rs` regression proving case-insensitive lookups still preserve dotted-vs-non-dotted key separation

Validation so far:
- `git diff --check -- src/net/dns/resolver.rs src/net/dns/cache.rs` PASS
- `ubs --only=rust src/net/dns` ran clean of new critical findings but is noisy on existing test unwrap/assert inventory and one unrelated lifecycle heuristic in resolver connect code
- `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets` reached remote `exit=0`; local tail then reported the usual artifact-retrieval vanished-file warning
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` is red outside this slice in `src/http/h1/http_client.rs`, `src/net/websocket/*`, and `tests/websocket_acceptor_trailing_bytes.rs`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` is red outside this slice in `src/stream/debounce.rs` and `src/net/websocket/handshake.rs`
- focused `rch` test lanes for the new DNS regressions are still running unusually slowly / quietly after compile on the remote worker; no DNS-specific failure has surfaced yet","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:20:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.4","title":"[audit][service] Reserve buffer capacity in poll_ready so clone races cannot revoke readiness","description":"Buffer::poll_ready currently reports Ready based only on the shared pending count, but it does not actually reserve a slot. Two clones can both observe Ready against capacity=1, after which one caller can fill the buffer and make the other caller's immediately-following call() fail with BufferError::Full. That violates the usual Service contract expectation that a successful poll_ready authorizes the next call on that handle. Fix by reserving a slot during poll_ready, consuming it in call(), releasing it on drop/close, and adding clone-race regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CloudyPond","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:50:03.548796130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T02:08:16.874795269Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T02:08:16.874685616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","buffer","service"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:50:03.548796130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.40","title":"[audit][trace] Keep TraceRecorder::take metadata rotation deterministic","description":"TraceRecorder::take() currently resets ReplayTrace metadata for the fresh recorder by overwriting recorded_at with SystemTime::now(). That reintroduces ambient wall-clock authority even when the caller supplied deterministic TraceMetadata, so repeated take()/rotate cycles produce nondeterministic trace headers. Patch src/trace/recorder.rs so recorder rotation preserves explicit metadata deterministically instead of consulting wall clock, add a focused regression around repeated take() behavior, and validate with rch-backed targeted check/clippy/test commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PinkForest","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-09T02:56:30.373627443Z","created_by":"PinkForest","updated_at":"2026-03-09T03:02:12.568558944Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T03:02:12.568533607Z","close_reason":"TraceRecorder::take no longer reintroduces ambient wall clock; deterministic rotation regression passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.40","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:56:30.373627443Z","created_by":"PinkForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.41","title":"[audit][trace] Make ReplayCheckpoint creation deterministic for identical trace positions","description":"ReplayCheckpoint::new currently stamps created_at from SystemTime::now(), so taking the same checkpoint twice against the same trace position yields different serialized checkpoint bytes and leaks ambient wall clock back into the deterministic replay toolchain. Patch src/trace/streaming.rs so checkpoint creation is deterministic for a given trace metadata + events_processed pair, add focused regression coverage for identical-position stability and monotonic differentiation across positions, and validate with rch-backed targeted check/clippy/test commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PinkForest","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-09T03:06:17.083408797Z","created_by":"PinkForest","updated_at":"2026-03-09T03:13:10.603716194Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T03:13:10.603691518Z","close_reason":"ReplayCheckpoint no longer uses ambient wall clock; identical-position checkpoint serialization is deterministic and targeted replay checkpoint tests passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","determinism","streaming","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.41","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T03:06:17.083408797Z","created_by":"PinkForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -2532,7 +2532,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","title":"Build current-thread live runner adapter for differential scenarios","description":"Create the live execution adapter that runs shared scenarios through real runtime code paths while minimizing gratuitous scheduling noise and preserving reproducibility.","design":"Build the live-side execution adapter that can take a shared scenario and drive the actual runtime paths under test with as little incidental nondeterminism as practical. Prefer current-thread or otherwise constrained runtime setups where that reduces scheduler noise without changing the semantics the surface is supposed to exercise.

The adapter should be explicit about what it controls and what it cannot control. It must not quietly turn live execution into another simulated path. The whole point is to run the real code paths while still making comparison practical.","acceptance_criteria":"A live-run adapter exists and can execute shared differential scenarios through real runtime code paths using a documented execution model rather than ad hoc per-suite setup.

Adapter bootstrapping, run lifecycle, and teardown behavior have direct unit or contract coverage plus at least one smoke-level e2e path using the same live-run surface the later pilots and scripts will use.

The adapter emits structured run metadata rich enough for later reports and replay guidance, including scenario identity, profile, seed lineage, runtime mode, and any known nondeterminism qualifiers.","notes":"This task is about controlling noise, not about hiding the fact that the live path is live.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:23:26.562404216Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T02:23:26.562379841Z","close_reason":"Implemented LiveRunnerAdapter: LiveRunnerConfig, LiveExecutionProfile, LiveWitnessCollector, LiveRunMetadata, LiveRunResult, run_live_adapter(). 10 new tests (total 80). Current-thread adapter with explicit witness collection, structured metadata logging, nondeterminism qualifiers, and full integration with DualRunHarness.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","live","runner","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:46.467178655Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:49.469850852Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:52.401530918Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","title":"Add live-side semantic capture hooks for comparison-relevant events","description":"Expose the semantic facts from live executions that the comparator needs, focusing on stable hooks rather than brittle raw traces or text logs.","design":"Instrument the live runtime just enough to recover the semantic facts defined by the comparator contract. That means capturing events and counters relevant to outcomes, cancellation lifecycle, loser drain, region quiescence, obligation settlement, and any surface-specific invariants chosen for early pilots.

Instrumentation should prefer stable semantic hooks over brittle log scraping or raw trace deltas. If a surface cannot be observed well enough yet, that should become a first-class limitation rather than an excuse to compare on shaky proxies.","acceptance_criteria":"The live execution path exposes the semantic evidence required by the normalized comparator schema through stable semantic hooks rather than fragile textual logs or incidental implementation details.

Missing observability is explicit and machine-readable rather than silently approximated, and capture-field behavior is covered by direct unit or fixture checks instead of being trusted only transitively.

The emitted evidence payloads and logs are structured enough that later normalization, reports, and crashpacks can trust the field names, missingness semantics, and provenance of each captured fact.","notes":"Better instrumentation beats more tolerant comparison logic. This task should bias toward improving evidence quality.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:03.391447578Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:24:31.630531292Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","instrumentation","live","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:58.499190711Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:03.391447578Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:55.386840494Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","title":"Normalize lab and live evidence into shared comparator records","description":"Build the actual normalization path that converts heterogeneous lab/live evidence into the same semantic record type for comparison.","design":"Implement the mapping that takes lab evidence and live evidence and collapses both into the canonical normalized record defined earlier. This includes canonical ordering for data that is semantically unordered, consistent naming for obligations and regions, and explicit elision of incidental detail.

Where the mapping is lossy, the loss must be intentional and justified. The project should be able to explain exactly why omitted detail is not needed to judge semantic parity for the chosen surface.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a concrete normalization path from both execution environments into the shared comparator record, and unit or fixture coverage exercises representative positive and negative mappings directly.

Canonical example mappings and golden record shapes are pinned, and field-level diff output plus structured diagnostics make false-positive and false-negative risk reviewable when normalization behavior changes.

At least one reusable downstream consumer beyond the local normalization tests uses the same normalized shape end-to-end, so later runner and reporting flows are not quietly relying on a different record contract.","notes":"This task is where semantic comparison becomes real instead of aspirational.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:07:49.279820374Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","observability","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:04.471910998Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:01.449827579Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","title":"Define time and scheduler-noise normalization policy for live re-execution","description":"Specify how virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise should be treated when comparing lab and live results so the framework stays semantically sharp without becoming brittle.","design":"Define how the framework reconciles virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise during live re-execution. Some surfaces will require ordering or timing windows to be normalized; others must reject timing-sensitive comparison entirely until the effect boundary is better controlled.

This task should set the explicit policy for when time-based facts are comparable, when they are only advisory, and when they are out of scope. It should also say how reruns interact with timing noise so the comparator does not accidentally ratchet itself into useless tolerance.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a written normalization policy for time-related and scheduler-noise-related evidence.

The policy makes it clear which time facts are part of semantic comparison and which are not.

The policy also defines how suppression, qualification, or explanation of scheduler noise appears in reports and verdict semantics so later CI and operator flows do not invent their own tolerance language.","notes":"This task exists because timing ambiguity is one of the fastest ways to destroy confidence in differential testing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:47:36.097031465Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","time","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:07.420718160Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:10.395240991Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:13.352500745Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","title":"Define time and scheduler-noise normalization policy for live re-execution","description":"Specify how virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise should be treated when comparing lab and live results so the framework stays semantically sharp without becoming brittle.","design":"Define how the framework reconciles virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise during live re-execution. Some surfaces will require ordering or timing windows to be normalized; others must reject timing-sensitive comparison entirely until the effect boundary is better controlled.

This task should set the explicit policy for when time-based facts are comparable, when they are only advisory, and when they are out of scope. It should also say how reruns interact with timing noise so the comparator does not accidentally ratchet itself into useless tolerance.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a written normalization policy for time-related and scheduler-noise-related evidence.

The policy makes it clear which time facts are part of semantic comparison and which are not.

The policy also defines how suppression, qualification, or explanation of scheduler noise appears in reports and verdict semantics so later CI and operator flows do not invent their own tolerance language.","notes":"Claimed after bv --robot-plan ranked the differential track as the clean unowned unblocker lane; implementing time and scheduler-noise normalization policy with contract coverage and downstream bindings.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteCrane","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:28:59.066860846Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","time","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:07.420718160Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:10.395240991Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:13.352500745Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","title":"Build live evidence capture and normalization unit suite with golden records","description":"Test the live-capture and normalization pipeline directly with deterministic fixtures, negative cases, and field-level mismatch diagnostics.","design":"Add the deterministic verification suite for live-side capture and normalization. This should cover positive and negative cases for evidence extraction, missing-observability behavior, canonical ordering, lossy normalization decisions, and representative golden records that prove lab and live evidence collapse into the intended shared shape.

The suite should also require detailed diff logging when normalization mismatches occur. If a normalization regression happens, the output should show exactly which semantic field diverged and which source evidence produced it.","acceptance_criteria":"Live evidence capture and normalization have dedicated unit/fixture coverage rather than being tested only transitively through later pilots.

Representative golden lab/live records are checked into the verification story and are easy to diff when behavior changes.

Normalization failures emit detailed field-level diagnostics that make root-cause analysis fast, and at least one targeted end-to-end path proves the same capture/normalization surface behaves correctly when exercised through the real runner flow.","notes":"Normalization is too central to leave under-tested. This bead makes it a first-class verification target instead of a hidden implementation detail.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T20:18:49.979386588Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:52:36.209905637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","golden","normalization","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:02.945870384Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:52:36.209849853Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:18:49.979386588Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:05.894942486Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:08.837063353Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:11.799783389Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","title":"Implement differential execution, divergence classification, and repro artifacts","description":"Assemble the execution engine that runs the same scenario through lab and live paths, classifies mismatches, emits minimized repro bundles when parity fails, and exposes reusable end-to-end runner/report entrypoints that make the system practical for humans and CI.","design":"This phase assembles the actual differential judge. Its job is to run both paths, compare normalized records, and preserve evidence aggressively when parity fails.

The design should treat repro quality as part of correctness. A differential test that only says "mismatch" without a seed, witness, or replay bundle creates more frustration than confidence. The system should therefore integrate minimization, classification, and artifact generation tightly rather than bolt them on after the fact.

This phase should also guard against false positives by distinguishing unsupported surfaces, missing observability, and scheduler-only noise from true semantic divergence.","acceptance_criteria":"A reusable differential runner exists and can execute the same scenario in lab and live paths.

Divergences are classified and emitted with enough artifact material to reproduce or minimize them.

Reusable e2e runner scripts exist and produce detailed structured logs plus a concise operator-facing summary/report.

The framework includes negative-control calibration coverage proving that intentional mismatches are detected, classified, and reported correctly rather than only passing on positive-parity cases.","notes":"Existing inspiration includes the differential-harness shape in `tests/raptorq_conformance.rs` plus replay/debugging workflows already documented in `docs/replay-debugging.md`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:08.972914180Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:47:39.050838654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["comparator","differential","epic","replay","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:08.972914180Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:17.103173971Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:20.073452489Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:23.019244933Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2oh2u.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:28.895500575Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3cddg.12.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:25.965022831Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","title":"Implement differential executor that runs each scenario in lab and live","description":"Build the shared execution engine that drives both runtimes from one scenario source and compares the resulting normalized semantic records.","design":"Implement the reusable executor that takes one scenario, runs the lab version and the live version, and then compares their normalized records. The runner should expose enough structure that higher-level suites can choose whether to stop on first divergence, retain all mismatches, or run a classification pass.

Avoid entangling this task with surface-specific semantics. The executor should be the plumbing that every pilot calls, while surface logic lives in scenario adapters and assertion helpers.","acceptance_criteria":"A reusable differential executor exists and is used by more than one surface-specific suite.

Unit/contract tests cover scenario dispatch, adapter invocation, and comparator wiring for the executor.

At least one smoke-level dual-run e2e path runs through the executor and emits structured run logs with seed/replay identifiers.","notes":"This is the structural center of the whole program. If it becomes surface-specific, the framework will fragment immediately.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:21.112469568Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:30:46.432971028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["comparator","differential","executor","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:16.307506012Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:25.167530662Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:28.107173951Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:19.266061418Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:22.228832130Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:21.112469568Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2oh2u.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:34.031057161Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3cddg.12.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:31.066563478Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
